classObjective: Write equations of lines in slope-intercept form that are parallel or perpendicular to a given line and pass through a given point.
workedExamples: 12-8, Example 3 — Determine Whether Lines Given by Equations Are Parallel, Perpendicular, or Neither
12-8, Example 4 — Graph Lines Using Parallel and Perpendicular Relationships
12-8, Example 5 — Write Equations for Parallel and Perpendicular Lines
warmUp: What is the slope of a line perpendicular to y = 2/3 x + 5?
conceptDevelopment: Mini-lesson: point-slope form for parallel/perpendicular; slope-intercept form; negative reciprocal slopes
guidedPractice: Teacher-led practice writing parallel and perpendicular line equations
independentPractice: Practice problems writing equations for lines parallel or perpendicular to given lines through specified points
assessment: Exit ticket: write the equation of the line perpendicular to y = -1/2 x + 3 passing through (4, 1)
capReflection: Why is the product of slopes of perpendicular lines equal to -1?
